title: 4 months of AI options trading: showing results and what I learned
author: Interesting_Rush_166
selftext:
Been manually trading options for about 2 years with mixed results. Was spending 3-4 hours daily watching charts and making emotional decisions that usually lost me money. Decided to try AI automation after burning through 5k in panic trades during a market dip.

Researched different platforms and ended up going with Cashflow AI since they had decent reviews and connected to my Schwab account easily. Put in 18k to test it out starting in September.

Results breakdown:

- September: slow start, up 1.8%
- October: gained momentum, up 6.2%
- November: election volatility helped, up 8.9%
- December: steady progress, up 4.1%
- Total return: 21.7% in 4 months

Biggest changes were mental. Stopped checking positions every 30 minutes. Sleep way better now. Weekends actually feel like downtime instead of worrying about Monday gaps.

The system handles earnings plays and market volatility much better than I ever did. During the Thanksgiving selloff I would have panic sold everything. The AI just kept executing its strategies and recovered quickly. Took about a month to fully trust letting it run without interference. Still get urges to override during red days but the data shows better performance when I stay hands off.

Anyone else using AI for options trading? Curious what platforms you've tried and how the results compare to manual trading

comments.author: FairFlowAI
comments.body:
Impressive sharing results from the future... 😅

* October: gained momentum, up 6.2%
* November: election volatility helped, up 8.9%

We are in AI trading as well, but focused on Future Markets... doesn't mean that I could share the next months profits ;-)

P.S.: "Human in the loop" is recommended here.

comments.author: ScienceAlive2865
comments.body: Nice work sticking with it for 4 months — most people quit after the first bad week. AI can help with structure, but risk, emotions, and discipline are still human problems. I’m building something called **TradeCanny**, an AI trading journal + coach that tracks patterns and mindset over time — if you’re curious, here’s a short survey: [https://forms.gle/WM4vgMTAHhfwkoiy6](https://forms.gle/WM4vgMTAHhfwkoiy6)

comments.author: InfluenceFeeling2786
comments.body: 4 months is a solid sample — way better than people who flex after a lucky week. The real value is in what broke, how you adjusted, and whether the system survived volatility or just looked good in trending markets. Keep sharing the lessons, not just the wins.
